"'''Revenants'''" was a comic written by [[W. Haden Blackman]]. It appeared in ''[[Star Wars Tales 18]]'' and was collected in ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 5]]''. It is set in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]].

==Plot summary==
[[File:Mandalorians_led_by_Boba_Fett.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]] led by [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]].]]

Several [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca's]] [[Death/Legends|death]], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] has been convinced by [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|his wife]] and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] to stop moping and go on a reconnaissance mission on the other side of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] from [[Vector Prime]].

Near [[Raxus Prime/Legends|Raxus Prime]], Han's shuttle encounters the [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']] and is shot down onto the junkyard [[Moon/Legends|moon]]. Keeping a log on his [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] for Leia, Han spends the next few weeks trekking across the moon, making makeshift weapons and survival gear to avoid [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], who is hunting him.

Han finally comes to the realization that there is more than one Fett following him. When that happens, he comes face to face with the real Fett. Fett reveals to Han that the entire event has been a training exercise to cull the weak recruits from the new batch of [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]] prior to them taking up service for the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]—since, as [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]], the Mandalorians go for the highest bidder, and the Yuuzhan Vong are currently the richest ones in the galaxy. The Mandalorians then take off, leaving Han behind.

==Continuity==
This story is referenced in ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 117]]'' and ''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''. However, the role of Han in it is questionable, since as of ''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' he and Fett had not met in a long time. However, Karen Traviss' e-book ''[[Boba Fett: A Practical Man]]'' confirms that Fett was purposely trying to mislead the galaxy into believing the Mandalorians were working for the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. This was a ruse to deceive [[Nom Anor]] and the Vong, so that Fett could learn about them and then strike at them from close range. It appears Han was at the wrong place at the wrong time, which allowed Fett to use him as a patsy who would spread the word of the Mandalorian's "defection" to the Vong.
